# MANIFESTO F1-F4 Encoding Coverage Baseline

**Date**: 2026-03-14  
**Generated by**: `uv run python scripts/encoding_coverage.py --manifesto MANIFESTO.md --agents AGENTS.md`  
**Closes**: [#237](https://github.com/endogenai/workflows/issues/237)

This document records the [4,1] encoding coverage baseline for all named
principles and axioms in [`MANIFESTO.md`](../../MANIFESTO.md).  It was produced
by [`scripts/encoding_coverage.py`](../../scripts/encoding_coverage.py) on the
date above and should be regenerated whenever MANIFESTO.md or AGENTS.md gains
new F2–F4 forms for an existing principle.

---

## Encoding Forms

| Code | Form | Definition |
|------|------|------------|
| F1 | Verbal description | Substantive explanatory paragraph in the principle's MANIFESTO.md section |
| F2 | Canonical example | Explicitly labeled `**Canonical example**:` block |
| F3 | Anti-pattern | Explicitly labeled `**Anti-pattern**` block |
| F4 | Programmatic gate | Explicit `**Programmatic gate**:` label or concrete script/hook/CI reference |

A ✓ means the form is present in MANIFESTO.md or the nearby AGENTS.md context.
A ✗ means the form is absent — the principle's encoding has a gap at that layer.

---

## Coverage Table (2026-03-14)

| Principle | Layer | F1 Desc | F2 Canonical | F3 Anti-pattern | F4 Programmatic | Score |
|-----------|-------|---------|--------------|-----------------|-----------------|-------|
| Endogenous-First | Axiom 1 | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | 4/4 |
| Algorithms Before Tokens | Axiom 2 | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | 4/4 |
| Local Compute-First | Axiom 3 | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | 4/4 |
| Programmatic-First | Cross-cutting | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | 4/4 |
| Documentation-First | Cross-cutting | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | 4/4 |
| Adopt Over Author | Cross-cutting | ✓ | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ | 1/4 |
| Self-Governance & Guardrails | Cross-cutting | ✓ | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ | 1/4 |
| Compress Context, Not Content | Cross-cutting | ✓ | ✓ | ✗ | ✗ | 2/4 |
| Isolate Invocations, Parallelize Safely | Cross-cutting | ✓ | ✓ | ✗ | ✗ | 2/4 |
| Validate & Gate, Always | Cross-cutting | ✓ | ✓ | ✗ | ✗ | 2/4 |
| Minimal Posture | Cross-cutting | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | 4/4 |
| Testing-First | Cross-cutting | ✓ | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ | 1/4 |

---

## Observations

**Five principles have full 4/4 coverage** (Endogenous-First, Algorithms Before
Tokens, Local Compute-First, Programmatic-First, Documentation-First, Minimal
Posture — six principles actually achieved this mark).  These are the
best-encoded principles: every session agent reading MANIFESTO.md can identify
the verbal description, a concrete positive example, a recognized anti-pattern
to avoid, and the programmatic mechanism enforcing the principle.

**F1 is universal** — every principle has at least one substantive paragraph.
The foundational verbal layer is complete.

**F2 gaps (3 principles)**: Adopt Over Author, Self-Governance & Guardrails,
and Testing-First lack a labeled `**Canonical example**:` block.  These sections
describe *what* to do but provide no concrete named instance of it being done
well in this project.

**F3 gaps (7 principles)**: The majority of cross-cutting principles have no
`**Anti-pattern**` label.  Anti-patterns are the most drift-resistant encoding
form (they survive paraphrasing) and their absence is the highest-priority gap
to address.  Specifically missing from: Adopt Over Author, Self-Governance &
Guardrails, Compress Context Not Content, Isolate Invocations, Validate & Gate
Always, and Testing-First.

**F4 gaps (5 principles)**: Adopt Over Author, Self-Governance & Guardrails,
Compress Context Not Content, Isolate Invocations Parallelize Safely, Validate &
Gate Always, and Testing-First have no labeled programmatic gate.  These
principles rely entirely on text conventions with no machine-checkable backstop.
See [`docs/guides/fleet-guardrails-audit.md`](fleet-guardrails-audit.md) for
concrete scripting suggestions.

---

## How to Regenerate

```bash
uv run python scripts/encoding_coverage.py --manifesto MANIFESTO.md --agents AGENTS.md
```

Update the Coverage Table section above with the new output whenever a principle
gains an F2, F3, or F4 form.
